reviewer.findings: C4 adds ntt_ct_gate: a dudect-style cycle-accurate constant-time gate (reusing the C4b sign.rs harness pattern: rdtsc, Welch's t, |t|<4.5 bar) covering (1) the NTT ring multiply now on the live path (poly_mul_ntt, including the C3 branchless red()) and (2) decaps valid-vs-invalid-ciphertext timing (FO re-encryption + implicit rejection). Ran --release (the mode the gate documents as authoritative, since the debug-only schoolbook cross-check in ring_mul would otherwise contaminate the measurement): ntt_ring_mul_is_constant_time |t|=0.1337, decaps_valid_vs_invalid_is_constant_time |t|=0.0790, both far under the 4.5 bar. gate_detects_deliberate_leak (sensitivity check on the known-leaky schoolbook poly_mul) measured |t|=1413.7, proving the harness can go RED. Full pq_kem suite: 14 passed, 1 ignored (decaps gate is debug-skipped by design), 0 failed.

overlap.findings: Verified the gate targets exactly what C2/C3 changed (ring_mul's poly_mul_ntt path, and red() now inside it) and nothing else invented. Confirmed the decaps test's #[cfg_attr(debug_assertions, ignore)] is intentional and documented (debug ring_mul's debug_assert_eq! runs the non-constant-time schoolbook oracle as common-mode noise that would falsely pass in debug and is skipped rather than silently green there — release is the only mode that reflects the shipping binary). Sensitivity check confirms the gate is not a rubber stamp: it demonstrably goes red on schoolbook poly_mul's data-dependent zero-skip.
